Section 1

🔐 Logging In

Login screen — Turkish (light mode)

Open the app URL. Enter the username and password provided by your administrator, then click Login / Giriş Yap.

❌ If you see Invalid username or password, double-check your credentials. Contact your system admin if the issue persists.
Section 2

🖥️ Interface Overview

Main interface — Turkish, light mode

Main interface — English, dark mode

After logging in you will see:

  • Sidebar (left panel) — language toggle, theme toggle, Start Over, Logout
  • Main area — four steps running top to bottom
  • Download buttons — appear after AI mapping is complete

Sidebar Controls

ControlWhat it does
🌐 TR / ENSwitch the entire UI and all output labels between Turkish and English
🌙 Dark / ☀️ LightToggle dark or light theme
🔄 Start Over / Yeniden BaşlaResets the page without clearing your session
🚪 Logout / ÇıkışReturns you to the login screen
1

Upload Your Exam File

Click the upload area (or drag and drop) to upload your exam.

Accepted formats: .txt  .docx  .doc  .pdf

The app scans the file and extracts questions automatically. You will see:

  • ✅ X questions found — extraction succeeded, click Preview to verify
  • ❌ No questions found — see Tips section below

How Questions Are Detected

The app recognises three common formats (tried in order):

Format A — Numbered with punctuation

1. What is the purpose of the Flatten() layer?
2. Which activation function is used for multi-class output?

Format B — Turkish "Soru" prefix

Soru 1. Aşağıdaki Keras kodu hangi tür katman ekler?
Soru 2. model.fit() çağrısında 'validation_split=0.2' ne anlama gelir?

Format C — Any line ending with ?

What does Conv2D(32, (3,3)) do?
Why is softmax used in the output layer?
💡 Important: Every question must end with a ? mark. Answer choices (A, B, C…) and answer keys are automatically stripped.
2

Define Learning Outcomes

You have two options — choose the tab that suits you:

Option A: Extract from PDF (Bilgi Paketi)

  1. Click the "Extract from PDF" tab
  2. Upload your course information package (Bilgi Paketi) PDF
  3. The AI reads the PDF and pulls out all learning outcomes automatically
  4. You will see them listed with their numbers and definitions
💡 This works best with standard university Bilgi Paketi documents. The AI looks for the Dersin Öğrenme Çıktıları section.

Option B: Enter Manually

  1. Click the "Enter Manually" tab
  2. Type or paste your learning outcomes, one per line
  3. Use the format: LO-1: definition text here
LO-1: Explains the fundamental concepts of convolutional neural networks
LO-2: Applies CNN models to image classification tasks
LO-3: Evaluates model performance using loss and accuracy metrics
LO-4: Designs and trains deep learning models using Keras

Once outcomes are loaded you will see: ✅ X outcomes defined

3

Set Scoring

Scoring, Answer Key and Rubric Form sections — Turkish, light mode

Total Exam Points

Set the total points for the exam (default: 100).

Scoring Type

OptionBehaviourExample
Equal (auto)Every question gets the same points100 ÷ 52 = 1.92 pts each
Custom per questionIndividual point box for each questionQ1=5, Q2=3, Q3=2…

With custom scoring, the app shows a running total:

  • ⚠️ Total: 98 — scores don't add up to the exam total yet
  • ✅ Total: 100 — all good
Optional

📝 Answer Key

Answer Key expander and Rubric Form Info — English, dark mode

Expand the Answer Key section and type the correct answers as a continuous string — no spaces or separators.

ABCDEABCDE  ← example for 10 questions

The answer key is embedded in the Excel report in the rightmost column of Sheet 1. Leave it blank if not needed.

Optional

📋 Rubric Form Details

Fill in the four fields to personalise the rubric DOCX header:

FieldTR LabelExample
InstructorÖğretim ElemanıÖğr. Gör. Hatice Tekiş
Exam TypeSınav TürüVize / Final / Quiz
Department / ProgramBölüm / ProgramBilgisayar Programcılığı
Exam DateSınav Tarihi01/06/2026

These appear at the top of the downloaded rubric document. All fields are optional.

4

Generate Report

Once Steps 1 and 2 are complete, the Map with AI & Generate Excel button becomes active. If either step is missing, you will see reminder warnings:

  • ⬆ Upload an exam file first
  • ⬆ Define learning outcomes first

Click the button. The AI processes questions in batches of 20. A progress bar shows which batch is running.

What the AI decides for each question

  • Which learning outcome(s) it maps to
  • If it spans multiple outcomes, what percentage belongs to each (always totals 100%)
  • Which Bloom's Taxonomy level it tests
  • Whether it is Easy, Medium, or Hard

When done: ✅ 52/52 questions mapped — 3 matched to multiple outcomes

Download Buttons

ButtonFileContents
⬇ Download Excel ReportExamName-Outcome-Report.xlsx3-sheet workbook
📄 Download Rubric FormExamName-Rubrik.docxPrint-ready rubric table
Section 9

📊 Understanding Your Outputs

Excel — Sheet 1: Question Map

ColumnDescription
Q No / Soru NoQuestion number
Score / PuanPoints for that question
Question / Soru MetniFull question text
LO Rank / ÖÇ SıraIndex of the matched learning outcome
Weight / Etki OranMatch percentage (sums to 100 across all matched LOs)
Bloom's TaxonomyCognitive level (Remember → Create)
Difficulty / ZorlukEasy / Medium / Hard
Answer KeyCorrect answer letter (if provided)

Excel — Sheet 2: LO Summary

Per-outcome overview: number of mapped questions and their question numbers. Useful for checking coverage balance.

Excel — Sheet 3: Proliz Format

Same data structured for the university Proliz accreditation system.

Rubric DOCX

A print-ready Word document containing the institution header, learning outcomes list, and a full scoring rubric table with Bloom level and difficulty per question.

Bloom's Taxonomy Levels

LevelTRTypical question cues
RememberHatırlama"What is…", "Which…", "Define…", "List…"
UnderstandAnlama"Explain…", "Describe…", "What does X mean…"
ApplyUygulama"Which code does X", "Calculate…", "Use X to…"
AnalyzeAnaliz"Compare…", "Why does X cause Y…", "Differentiate…"
EvaluateDeğerlendirme"Which approach is better…", "Justify…", "Assess…"
CreateYaratma"Design…", "Build…", "Formulate…", "Propose…"

Difficulty Levels

LevelTRMeaning
EasyKolayStraightforward recall or recognition
MediumOrtaRequires understanding or simple application
HardZorRequires analysis, evaluation, or creation
Section 10

🌐 Language & Theme

Switching Language

Use the TR / EN toggle in the sidebar. Affects everything:

  • All UI text and labels
  • Excel column headers (ÖÇ Sıra ↔ LO Rank)
  • LO prefix in outputs (ÖÇ-4(%100) ↔ LO-4(%100))
  • Bloom labels (Hatırlama ↔ Remember)
  • Difficulty labels (Kolay ↔ Easy)
  • Rubric DOCX headers and section titles
💡 Switch language before running the mapping so that the downloaded files use your preferred language.

Dark / Light Mode

Click the 🌙 Dark or ☀️ Light button in the sidebar. Visual preference only — does not affect output files.

Section 11

🛠️ Tips & Common Issues

❌ No questions found

  • Make sure every question ends with ?
  • Try converting your file to .txt and re-uploading
  • If using a PDF, check that the text is selectable (not a scanned image)
  • For DOCX files with tables, the app reads table cells automatically

❌ No outcomes found (PDF extraction)

  • Make sure the PDF is a standard Bilgi Paketi with a Dersin Öğrenme Çıktıları section
  • If extraction fails, switch to the Enter Manually tab

⚠️ Custom scoring total doesn't match

  • The sum of all per-question scores must equal the Total Exam Points
  • Adjust individual scores until you see ✅ Total: 100

⏳ AI mapping seems slow

  • The AI processes 20 questions per batch — a 60-question exam runs 3 batches
  • Each batch typically takes 5–15 seconds
  • Do not close or reload the tab while mapping is running

📥 Download buttons disappeared

  • Click 🔄 Start Over and re-run the mapping

🔁 Re-run with different outcomes

  • Update your outcomes in Step 2, then click Map with AI again
⚠️ For technical issues or access problems, contact your system administrator.
by Lecturer Hatice Tekiş  ·  Kapadokya University  ·  Accreditation Report System
